3.happening: Count the {len(preorder)} nodes the tree will have.
3.why: preorder lists every node exactly once.

4.happening: Start an empty list of tree nodes.
4.why: We create node objects as we go, and draw them.

5.happening: Start a lookup from node id to node object.
5.why: Linking children needs O(1) access by id.

6.why: It records each node as unseen, building, or done — driving the colors.

7.happening: Loop over every future node to initialize its state.
7.invariant: Slots below i are already set.

9.happening: Start a map from value to its inorder index.
9.why: An O(1) lookup replaces scanning inorder each time.

10.why: We record where every value sits.
10.invariant: Positions below i are already mapped.

11.happening: Record that {inorder[i]} sits at inorder index {i}.
11.why: This lets us split subtrees in O(1) later.
11.invariant: Each value maps to its inorder index.

12.happening: Create a one-slot holder for the next preorder position.
12.why: Preorder is consumed left-to-right as roots.
12.invariant: pre_idx[0] is the next root's position.

13.happening: Build the whole tree over the full inorder range.
13.why: The first preorder value is the overall root.

14.happening: Serialize the finished tree into level-order form.
14.why: LeetCode expects the tree as a level-order array.

15.why: The tree is fully built and serialized.

18.happening: The inorder range is empty ({left} > {right}) — no node here.
18.why: An empty range means a missing child.

18.happening: The inorder range [{left}..{right}] is non-empty — make its root.
18.why: A non-empty range has a subtree root.

19.happening: Return None — this child is absent.
19.why: Nothing to build for an empty range.

20.happening: The next preorder value (position {pre_idx[0]}) becomes this subtree's root.
20.why: Preorder visits roots before their subtrees.
20.invariant: node_id is the current root's id.

21.happening: Read the root's value from preorder.
21.why: We need it to place the node and split inorder.

22.happening: Advance the preorder pointer past this root.
22.why: The next call takes the following preorder value.

23.why: A new tree node pops in.
23.invariant: A fresh node has no children yet.

24.happening: Add the new node to the tree.
24.why: It becomes visible in the drawing.

25.why: Children will attach to it by id.

26.happening: Mark node {node_id} building (on the call stack).
26.why: Amber marks the active construction path.

27.happening: This node has a parent (id {parent_id}) to attach to.
27.why: Every node except the root links to a parent.
27.invariant: Only the overall root has no parent.

27.happening: This is the overall root — no parent to attach.

28.happening: It is the parent's LEFT child.
28.why: The parent asked for its left subtree here.

28.happening: It is the parent's RIGHT child.
28.why: The parent asked for its right subtree here.

29.happening: Attach node {node_id} as node {parent_id}'s left child.
29.why: The edge now appears in the tree.
29.invariant: The parent's left link points here.

31.happening: Attach node {node_id} as node {parent_id}'s right child.
31.why: The edge now appears in the tree.
31.invariant: The parent's right link points here.

32.happening: Find the root's index in inorder — it splits the range into left and right.
32.why: Values before it are the left subtree; values after it are the right.
32.invariant: middle is the root's inorder index.

33.happening: Build the left subtree from the inorder range before the root.
33.why: Those values sit left of the root in inorder.
33.invariant: The left child is built before the right (preorder).

34.happening: Build the right subtree from the inorder range after the root.
34.why: Those values sit right of the root in inorder.
34.invariant: The right child is built after the left.

35.happening: Both subtrees built — mark node {node_id} done.

36.why: The parent records it as a child.

39.why: We serialize the finished tree to return it.

40.happening: Start a queue to walk the tree breadth-first.
40.why: Level order is a BFS of the tree.

41.happening: The tree is non-empty — seed the walk with the root.
41.why: Level order starts at the root.

41.happening: The tree is empty — nothing to serialize.
41.why: An empty tree serializes to an empty array.

43.why: We serialize until the queue empties.

44.happening: Take the next id from the front.
44.why: BFS emits the earliest-queued id first.

45.happening: This slot is a missing child.
45.why: Level order records holes as null.
45.invariant: Missing children still take a slot.

45.happening: This id points at a real node.
45.invariant: A real id has a value.

46.happening: Emit null for the missing child.

48.why: Level order lists values top-down, left-to-right.

49.happening: Queue its left child to emit next.
49.why: Children follow their parent in level order.

50.happening: Queue its right child after the left.
50.why: Right follows left within a level.

52.why: It is not part of the answer.

init.happening: Setting up: counting nodes and indexing inorder.
init.why: An index map makes each split O(1).

recurse.why: Each call builds one subtree root, then its children.
recurse.invariant: The call stack traces the current path.

check.happening: Testing a range or a child side.
check.why: Empty ranges make no node; the side decides the link.

move.why: A finished subtree hands its root to the parent.

update.happening: Creating a node, linking it, or splitting inorder.
update.why: This is where the tree grows.
update.invariant: State and links reflect the work done.

return.happening: Serializing and returning the finished tree.

loop.happening: Walking the tree breadth-first to serialize it.
loop.why: Level order is a BFS emit.
